cases.passage: India is one of the largest exporters of IT services in the world. Many Indian companies like Infosys and Wipro have set up their offices in countries like the United States, United Kingdom, and Australia to cater to the growing demand for IT services globally. These companies outsource their work to other countries like the Philippines and Vietnam, where labor costs are lower. This has led to the growth of IT industry in these countries and has created employment opportunities for thousands of people. However, it has also raised concerns about the impact of outsourcing on the Indian economy.

questions.q: What is one of the major reasons for Indian IT companies to set up their offices in foreign countries?
opts: (A) To cater to the growing demand for IT services globally
(B) To reduce labor costs
(C) To increase their market share
(D) To diversify their business
questions.explanation: Indian IT companies set up their offices in foreign countries to cater to the growing demand for IT services globally.

questions.q: What is the impact of outsourcing on the Indian economy?
opts: (A) It has led to the growth of IT industry in India
(B) It has created employment opportunities for thousands of people
(C) It has raised concerns about the impact of outsourcing on the Indian economy
(D) It has increased the trade deficit of India
questions.explanation: Outsourcing has raised concerns about the impact of outsourcing on the Indian economy.

questions.q: Which of the following countries is not a major export market for Indian IT services?
opts: (A) United States
(B) United Kingdom
(C) China
(D) Australia
questions.explanation: China is not a major export market for Indian IT services.

questions.q: What is the primary motivation behind Indian IT companies outsourcing their work to other countries?
opts: (A) To reduce labor costs
(B) To increase their market share
(C) To diversify their business
(D) To increase their profits
questions.explanation: The primary motivation behind Indian IT companies outsourcing their work to other countries is to reduce labor costs.

questions.q: What is the result of the growth of IT industry in countries like the Philippines and Vietnam?
opts: (A) Unemployment
(B) Increase in import of IT products
(C) Employment opportunities for thousands of people
(D) Decrease in IT industry
questions.explanation: The growth of IT industry in countries like the Philippines and Vietnam has created employment opportunities for thousands of people.

cases.passage: The European Union (EU) is a prime example of a trade bloc that has implemented policies to promote free trade among its member states. The EU has eliminated tariffs and non-tariff barriers on goods traded within the bloc, creating a single market for goods and services. This has led to increased trade and economic integration among its member states, resulting in the creation of a large and competitive market. However, this has also raised concerns about the impact on smaller businesses and the environment.

questions.q: What is one of the main objectives of the European Union in promoting free trade among its member states?
opts: (A) To increase tariffs and non-tariff barriers
(B) To promote economic integration among its member states
(C) To protect smaller businesses and the environment
(D) To reduce trade among its member states
questions.explanation: The main objective of the European Union in promoting free trade among its member states is to promote economic integration among its member states.

questions.q: What is the result of the elimination of tariffs and non-tariff barriers on goods traded within the EU?
opts: (A) Decrease in trade and economic integration
(B) Increase in trade and economic integration
(C) No impact on trade and economic integration
(D) Increase in tariffs and non-tariff barriers
questions.explanation: The elimination of tariffs and non-tariff barriers on goods traded within the EU has led to increased trade and economic integration among its member states.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of the single market created by the EU?
opts: (A) Reduced trade and economic integration
(B) Increased trade and economic integration
(C) Protection of smaller businesses and the environment
(D) Decrease in competitiveness
questions.explanation: The single market created by the EU has led to increased trade and economic integration among its member states.

questions.q: What is one of the concerns raised by the implementation of policies to promote free trade within the EU?
opts: (A) Increase in tariffs and non-tariff barriers
(B) Decrease in trade and economic integration
(C) Protection of smaller businesses and the environment
(D) Increase in unemployment
questions.explanation: One of the concerns raised by the implementation of policies to promote free trade within the EU is the protection of smaller businesses and the environment.

questions.q: What is a result of the creation of a large and competitive market within the EU?
opts: (A) Decrease in trade and economic integration
(B) Increase in unemployment
(C) Increase in trade and economic integration
(D) Decrease in competitiveness
questions.explanation: The creation of a large and competitive market within the EU has led to increased trade and economic integration among its member states.
